codeReview.summary: Solid implementation of WalkForwardRunner with correct sliding window generation, per-window cascade execution, delta/summary computation, verdict logic, and Schema 4E conformance. 608 lines of implementation, 1013 lines of tests (39 test cases). All core requirements met: calendar-month window boundaries, configurable train/test/step, per-window isolation via separate EvaluationRunner instances, correct zero-division handling (delta_pct=null when train_metric=0), three-tier verdict system, and graceful no-data window skip.

issues.description: VAL-WF-009 (River adapter multi-month data) has no test in this feature's test file. The handoff claims coverage of VAL-WF-001 through VAL-WF-011, but no test exercises the River adapter path. This is mitigated by: (1) the walk-forward module is data-source agnostic (takes bars_by_tf dict), (2) River adapter tested separately in its own feature, (3) cross-area integration test VAL-XA-002 covers the River→walk-forward pipeline.

Imports private function _filter_bars_by_date from ra.evaluation.runner. This creates a cross-module dependency on a private API. If runner.py renames or changes this function, walk_forward.py will silently break. Consider making it a shared utility or a public function.

std_test_metric uses population standard deviation (divides by N) rather than sample std (N-1). This is internally consistent and used correctly in tests, but differs from numpy's default np.std(ddof=0). Not a bug since the feature doesn't specify which to use, and the tests verify the exact values.

When delta_pct is None (train_metric=0), passed defaults to True. This is a reasonable design choice but not explicitly specified in the feature description or validation contract. Edge case behavior is documented only by test coverage.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The walk_forward module imports a private function _filter_bars_by_date from runner.py. This cross-module private API dependency is not documented in AGENTS.md. Multiple evaluation modules may need date filtering — consider documenting this shared utility pattern or promoting it to a public function in the evaluation package.

Worker followed procedure correctly. Skill feedback suggests adding a note that test_evaluation_runner.py takes >120s and can be excluded with --ignore during full-suite validation. Worker used this workaround successfully (ran 555 tests excluding runner tests, plus regression tests separately).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback.suggestedChanges: 'Skill could mention test_evaluation_runner.py takes >120s and suggest --ignore for full-suite validation'. Transcript shows timeout on full suite, then successful runs with --ignore.

summary: PASS. The walk-forward-validation feature is well-implemented with 608 lines of production code and 1013 lines of tests (39 cases). It correctly implements: sliding window generation with calendar-month boundaries, per-window cascade execution with isolation (fresh EvaluationRunner per window), delta/delta_pct computation with zero-division handling, summary statistics (mean, std, worst window), three-tier verdict logic (STABLE/CONDITIONALLY_STABLE/UNSTABLE), no-data window skipping, and Schema 4E conformance. The main gap is VAL-WF-009 (River multi-month test) which is absent from this feature's tests but covered by cross-area integration. Four non-blocking issues identified: missing River test, private function import coupling, population vs sample std choice, and undocumented delta_pct=null→passed=True behavior.
